message:
feat(ft-xbnl0.2.3): cx-first shutdown-bridge poll sleep (tick 285)

Migrates the snapshot engine's shutdown-bridge spawned task (main.rs
L15527) to use `sleep_with_cx` with cancel-forwarding:

- `runtime_compat::sleep(Duration::from_millis(500))` →
  `sleep_with_cx(&bridge_cx, Duration::from_millis(500))` with Err-handling
  that forwards a shutdown signal to the periodic loop via
  `snap_shutdown_tx.send(true)` and breaks.

This task bridges the AtomicBool shutdown flag into a watch channel
consumed by the snapshot engine's `run_periodic` loop. Previously the
500ms polling slice was cx-insensitive — a cx-cancel on the outer
`ft watch` runtime couldn't propagate here because the task was
spawned without cx plumbing and used ambient sleep.

Now the bridge task respects cx-cancel: a cancelled Cx → sleep returns
Err → we forward the shutdown signal → periodic loop wakes and
terminates. Symmetric to the AtomicBool poll semantics.

Pattern mirrors tick 282 (`distributed_agent_sleep_with_shutdown`) and
tick 283 (mux + watcher poll sleeps): cx-cancel treated as shutdown-
equivalent, flowing into existing termination paths.

Verification: `cargo check -p frankenterm --bin ft` compiles clean.

Cumulative ft-xbnl0.2.3 ticks this session: 99 (187-285).
main.rs cross-crate migration total: 180 sites (179 before + 1).
frankenterm-core internal sites: 47 (unchanged this tick).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
